Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Since the scenario is about a product design and launch process, we can assume some typical declarative constraints.

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Lets list the activities again with their abbreviations for reference: IG: Idea Generation DD: Design Draft TFC: Technical Feasibility Check CE: Cost Evaluation PC: Prototype Creation LT: Laboratory Testing UT: User Testing AG: Approval Gate MP: Marketing Plan FL: Final Launch

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Well consider the following constraints some examples but we can add more: 1. Existence constraints... 9. Non-coexistence...

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However note: The problem does not specify the exact constraints so we will make reasonable assumptions for a typical process. Lets assume the following constraints for this scenario: - Init: The process starts with IG. - Existence: FL must occur at least once since we launch the product. - Exactly one: IG occurs exactly once one idea per process instance. - Response: After IG we must have DD Design Draft at some point. - Precedence: FL Final Launch can only occur after AG Approval Gate has occurred. - Chain response: DD must be immediately followed by TFC Technical Feasibility Check. chain response: if DD occurs then TFC must occur immediately after - Chain precedence: PC Prototype Creation must be immediately preceded by TFC Technical Feasibility Check and CE Cost Evaluation?? Actually lets think: Typically after feasibility and cost evaluation we create the prototype.

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Alternatively we can have: For PC Prototype Creation we might want it to be preceded by both TFC and CE but not necessarily immediately. So we can use precedence constraints for that. Lets break down: We can have: precedence: TFC -> PC PC can only occur if TFC has occurred before precedence: CE -> PC PC can only occur if CE has occurred before

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets reconsider the process flow: IG -> DD -> TFC -> CE -> PC -> LT -> UT -> AG -> MP -> FL But that is one possible path. DECLARE allows flexibility so we can set constraints that allow multiple orders. Lets set: init: IG existence: FL exactly_one: IG response: IG DD precedence: AG FL chainresponse: DD TFC chainresponse: TFC CE chainresponse: CE PC chainresponse: PC LT chainresponse: LT UT chainresponse: UT AG chainresponse: AG MP chainresponse: MP FL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But note: for binary constraints the key is a tuple of two activities. The problem statement might be a bit ambiguous but in pm4py for binary constraints the key is a tuple activity1 activity2. Lets design the constraints one by one. Well set support to 1.0 for all constraints as per the problem statement and confidence to 1.0 as well assuming strict constraints. Note: The problem does not require us to include all constraint types but we can include only the ones we need. Well include: init existence exactly_one response precedence chainresponse Lets leave others empty or we can not include them but the problem says the model is expressed as a dictionary with these keys so we should include all keys but with empty dictionaries for the ones we dont use. model = {existence: {} absence: {} exactly_one: {} init: {} responded_existence: {} coexistence: {} response: {} precedence: {} succession: {} altresponse: {} altprecedence: {} altsuccession: {} chainresponse: {} chainprecedence: {} chainsuccession: {} noncoexistence: {} nonsuccession: {} nonchainsuccession: {} } Now fill in: init: IG -> IG: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 existence: FL -> FL: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 exactly_one: IG -> IG: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 response: IG DD -> IG DD: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 precedence: AG FL -> AG FL: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 chainresponse: DD TFC: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 TFC CE: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 CE PC: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 PC LT: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 LT UT: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 UT AG: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 AG MP: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0 MP FL: support: 1.0 confidence: 1.0

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that the problem says for the binary constraints the value is a dictionary with keys being the activities as a tuple and value the support and confidence. So for chainresponse we would have a dictionary with multiple entries.
